transcript.text: Hello.
Good, how are you?
Good.
I don't know why.
Can you see me?
I don't know.
I just see a purple screen with a K on it.
Oh, I know why, because I'm still in a different call.
I was in a Microsoft meeting.
It didn't close.
There you go.
Yeah, it's going to be me today.
Ah, okay.
I know Thiago messaged.
He said he'd be watching this later.
So I'll just be going over some stuff.
think Mara had a medical appointment, so she might be late.
Either way, I think we can get started and get going with this.
Yeah.
Okay, so just starting off with the content updates.
We'll have 400 articles published by this Saturday.

I wanted to flag, I was working with the two website developers that Datagrid had hired, Noor and Tejas, I think one of them left, Noor left, and Tejas is taking over, I think there was a bit of a handover issue, so Tejas, I think he's catching up with some of the things that Noor was doing.
Yeah, Noor just sent a note, I think it was yesterday, of all this stuff, so Tejas is now getting all caught up.
Okay, so anyway, so I'm catching up with them, and so that's kind of where the use case and industry pages are locked right now.
Okay.
And then I think there was one more task that Tiago had flagged earlier, which was updating the main block categories.
So there were some main categories that were created in the CMS, just to reduce the clutter a little bit.
We have the maps and ready, it's just that Tejas had told me the plan needs to get upgraded.
or something.
So just waiting on that.
So that's where that's at.
And then we have automated publishing into Datagrid's CMS.
So we're not using external publishers anymore.
So that's been ongoing.
There's nothing to do on your end.
I think there was one thing that Renato had to change that was all handled.
And it's all good to go.
So those are the general updates.
Just going through the performance report real quick.
I'm saying real quick because there is a deeper dive into the keywords that I want to get into.
But generally, this week, we saw a significant rise in impressions, which is this turquoise line over here.
We've been seeing this across all accounts.
And there's a couple things that we're sort of playing around with.
One is that perhaps the data from Google Search Console is not that great, which is something we've been seeing for the past.
A few weeks for some weird reason.
The other thing is like AI overviews over the past few weeks have expanded significantly.

And what that has done is all the sort of companies and results that used to be in the first page, sorry, the first sort of position or the second position, that real estate has been taken up by the AI overviews, which now takes up almost half the page.
So as a result of that, impressions skyrocket and clicks decrease.
So that's why we're seeing this kind of thing happening where the impressions are going almost like exponential in this case, like all of a sudden.
And, you know, clicks are taking a while to catch up.
So we're still observing this over the next few weeks just to see how this plays out.
So, yeah, but as of now, you know, the impressions increasing like this is not bad news or anything of that sort.
It gives the brand more visibility.
It's just that a click is not… Um,
Obviously, a click used to be far more valuable than an impression, but that's not quite the case.
So, yeah.
Okay.
Just an update on the newer clusters that we've been publishing over the past four weeks.
So, you know, the clicks aren't high as yet, but the impressions are taking off, which is good.
And then, obviously, as we publish more and more, we're going to see more traction in these particular articles.
Some of the things that we're seeing is that a few pages are ranking well already.
We're in the top 10 in quite a few, and we're on the cusp with others.
And more importantly, the keywords that are showing up, I don't know if I can zoom in on this.
No.
Sorry, I'm on a Mac.
I'm not quite sure how to do this.

But anyway, I know you can't see this, but some of the keywords we're showing up for is like AI CRM automation for financial advisors, AI tools for financial advisors, AI agent implementation for financial planning, etc.
AI agents.
Content info, construction change order, AI agent.
These are very specific queries.
So this is pretty good news to see this kind of keyword data coming through.
Because in Q1, we weren't seeing this kind of granular searches showing up.
So that's pretty good news.
And then Tiago had mentioned, sorry, messaged me a few hours back just saying that he wanted to dive deeper into the clicks, etc.
So I had sent him this particular report.
This is basically just pulled from the looker data, local report data that we have, and just ran some basic data analysis on this.
And I've shipped the Excel file here, which has the raw data as well.
We're just going to run through some of the charts over here and just some of the implications and conclusions I've been drawing from it.
So the first chart really just shows the position, distribution, and visibility.
Which is, you know, where are we showing up in Google and how often are we showing up?
So positions one to three, we have about 70 keywords, you know, that we're acting for in the top three.
And it's about 2,700 impressions.
So that's how often we're showing up over there, which is pretty good news.
The, you know, bulk of impressions are coming from positions four to ten.
And we have around 468 keywords over there.
Positions 11 to 20, obviously, you know, the reason the impressions are so high is because the higher volume of keywords that are in that position.
This distribution is quite normal.
The keywords that were showing up in positions one to three, I'll cover this shortly, but generally it's around the words like automation, AI agents, workflow automation, this kind of thing.
So it's pretty well targeted.
So that's good news.
of of bit Thank
The next section is just the overall visibility for automation as such, and this is only around searches for automation.
We're getting about 31,500 total impressions across all keywords so far, and then the keywords that contain automate or automation, more than 15,000 plus impressions.
And, you know, generally the search queries with automation, the reason I'm spotlighting this is because that's kind of what we're targeting.
Generally, searches across insurance automation is quite tight.
We are lagging, though, in broader financial services automation, and that's something we're hoping to cover with the new content we're publishing because we do have a few categories over there.
In terms of use cases, the highest visibility so far has been around document processing, workflow automation, data entry, competitor tracking.
The competitor tracking is...
Threw me off a bit.
I'm still digging into that just to see which exact keywords we're talking about.
But generally, you know, this is kind of what we expected when we started.
So it's kind of showing that things are working.
In terms of landing pages, as in like the actual block pages we've created, insurance document processing has massive visibility.
That's the top.
Competitor tracking, again, I'm surprised this is number two, but that's where we're at.
And insurance, again, at number three and claim form processing at number five.
So, you know, generally, this is why we were pushing for the insurance industry page, because we're seeing a lot of this, these types of searchers show up.
So that seems to be pretty promising in terms of what at least what Google is showing us.
Sorry, I know I've been talking a lot.
Can I take any questions or can I answer something?
So you said you were digging into more of why competitor tracking?
Has that one been ranked before?
you said into more.
Is this or is this just in this last week?
This is like a cumulative view.
this is from the start of January.
Yeah, so it's like total.
So then just breaking it down by categories.
So basically what this chart shows is like the keywords that are mapping two pages within these particular categories.
So productivity tools are showing up quite high.
And again, this is because we're talking about stuff like document handling, improving processes, and this kind of thing.
So that's why it's coming under productivity.
So generally we can see like AI automation productivity tools are the most popular.
Insurance handling, this is more to do with a very specific use case.
So it's one subset of the broader insurance sort of pillars that we've been targeting.
Generally, and then in terms of the...
Landing pages in terms of the impressions and the number of times we're showing up, document processing and insurance, insurance workflows, AI agents, usage for report writing, and entry automation.
We're showing up a lot for these particular things.
So, again, this is pretty much what we expected, and it's quite encouraging to see that, you know.
Is this graph also supposed to show clicks and there's just none, or is it?
Yeah, I was going to spotlight that.
I was like, wait, are there no clicks, just impressions on this?
Yeah, the reason those clicks are not showing up is because the Search Console data that we've been receiving for clicks has been a little weird.
It's showing us, for the past two, three weeks, it's been showing us zero clicks on everything, which is clearly wrong.
So these charts are created based on those zero clicks.
That's why it's showing zero everywhere.
Okay, but there things.
Okay.
There are things.
There are
Yeah, just don't have that.
have the numbers.
Okay.
Got it.
So I just left a note over here just spotlighting that, which is like, you know, click ticker has been a bit poor.
Yeah.
It's kind of messing up my chart.
But anyway, in terms of the, you know, we also looked at like the intent behind the queries themselves as in what is it that they're asking for.
So generally, you know, this is just to see like what people are searching for.
So again, in line with the data above, they're searching for things around automating stuff, you know, using AI agents or something, integrations, improving workflows.
And then we have a few words like how to best what, how can, etc.
Just problem solving.
But the far and away winner is basically the automation queries, which is, again, you know, everything that we've covered so far sort of of validates that.
And then in

In terms of the keyword clusters themselves, like looking at the different keywords, not the landing pages, but just the keywords themselves and what they are covering and, you know, what is it that people are trying to do in this case within the keywords.
It's pretty well spread.
mean, we're seeing insurance automation again with pretty high sort of coverage there.
Integrations and report writing are closed second, and then we have data entry and competitive tracking.
So this pretty closely matches to the landing page data that we were saying.
So the key takeaway from all of this and all of my talking so far is that, you know, generally the more specialized and technical automation topics are ranking better.
Content that is really tailored towards one granular use case is doing very well.
One reason for this is the construction industry and the insurance industry, both cases.
We have, like, really granular content that's digging into actual workflows.
They're writing really well.
So generally, you know, tailoring our content to Datagrid's ideal audience that we're trying to figure out via the JTPD workstream that George has been running.

I think once we get data from there and we get some conclusions from there, it should be really helpful for us in terms of really just narrowing things down much more and we can get, like, better results.
So that was the keyword sort of deep dive over here.
You know, obviously, there's a lot in this, so please do take a look and always happy to answer questions.
But, yeah, that was kind of the keyword deep dive that I sort of tagged on to this call.
Any questions about that?
Anything else I can answer about the performance generally?
Any questions?
questions?
Any Any questions?
Any questions?
Any questions?
questions?
Any Any questions?
Any Any questions?
questions?
questions?
It's good.
Thank you.
Okay.
And then to go on to link building, it's continuing as we planned.
Some of our articles, especially the ones with statistics, et cetera, are attracting backlinks.
With regards to the talk leadership, I know Mara has been leading that, so that's going on as planned.
I think Thiago has been interacting with the agency working on it pretty actively over there.
So with the JTBD work stream and the, you know, George and Jason, I know they're scheduling time with Thiago to just refine it a bit following a call last week.
And George had sent through a Notion page just outlining the process of the deliverables, et cetera, over there.
So that's the major, those are the major updates for this week.
I'm happy to answer any questions you might have.
I don't have any questions now.
So thank you for this.
Okay.
That was pretty much it.
Oh, hey, Mara.
Hey!
Sorry to run a little late, running from a different meeting, but that was super helpful.
Thank you for pulling together all of that reporting.
I know.
It's nice to not realize it in that way.
I don't know if that helps you, Kaitlin, but for me, I feel like the pie chart is the perfect representation.
Yeah.
Cool.
Awesome.
So, yeah, that was pretty much it this week.
As always, happy to answer any questions and follow-ups in Slack.
Yeah.
And then just let me know.
So you said with Tejas and he's just a little bit behind, but let me know if that still gets stalled and I can put some pressure there to get it organized.
And I think we're looking at a few other people on Upwork as well, too.
Okay.
Sounds good.
I'll give him a couple of days.
So, yeah, he's been pretty responsive so far.
But yeah.
Very good.
I think he also has a few other people that work for him, so we could also probably reach out to him about that.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Yeah.
Anything from you, Mara?
I don't think so.
I'll just, I know Tiago is traveling today.
I'll just kind of, I'm sure he's reviewing the, oh, sorry about that, the longer jobs to be done memo, but I'll just kind of bump that.
Maybe today or tomorrow a.m.
Sorry, I don't know why I'm getting all these slacks, but I will bump him tomorrow a.m.
just to make sure that he has reviewed, if he has any other feedback that we should apply and get it, another session booked as needed.
Okay, great.
Well, let me know if you need any help with that, and I appreciate it, guys.
Sounds good.
All right.
Talk to you later.
Thanks.
Bye, y Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Datagrid and GrowthX to discuss content strategy, SEO performance, and ongoing initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content production on track: 400 articles to be published by Saturday](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=56.0)
  - [SEO performance showing positive trends: significant rise in impressions, specialized/technical topics ranking well](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=155.0)
  - [JTBD workstream progressing: George & Jason scheduling refinement call with Thiago](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=868.0)
  - [Website dev transition: Noor left, Tejas catching up; some tasks pending upgrade](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)

## Topics

### Content & Website Updates

  - [400 articles to be published by Saturday](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=56.0)
  - [Website dev transition: Noor left, Tejas catching up](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)
  - [Use case and industry pages currently locked](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=92.0)
  - [Main block categories update pending plan upgrade](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=108.0)
  - [Automated publishing into Datagrid's CMS implemented](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=124.0)

### SEO Performance

  - [Significant rise in impressions (turquoise line in graph)](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=155.0)
  - [Clicks lagging due to AI overviews taking up SERP real estate](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [New clusters (4 weeks old) showing promise: low clicks, but impressions taking off](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
  - [Top-performing keywords: AI CRM automation, AI tools for financial advisors, AI agent implementation](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=283.0)
  - [70 keywords ranking in positions 1-3, 468 in positions 4-10](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=366.0)
  - [High visibility for insurance automation; lagging in broader financial services automation](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)
  - [Top use cases: document processing, workflow automation, data entry, competitor tracking](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=469.0)
  - [Top landing pages: insurance document processing, competitor tracking, insurance, claim form processing](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=491.0)

### Keyword Deep Dive

  - [Specialized and technical automation topics ranking better](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=754.0)
  - [Granular, use case-specific content performing well (e.g., construction, insurance industries)](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=773.0)
  - [Keyword intent analysis: automation queries dominate](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=707.0)
  - [Keyword clusters: insurance automation, integrations, report writing, data entry, competitive tracking](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=735.0)

### Link Building & Thought Leadership

  - [Ongoing as planned](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=843.0)
  - [Statistics-based articles attracting backlinks](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=849.0)
  - [Mara leading thought leadership efforts; Thiago actively involved with agency](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=854.0)

### JTBD Workstream

  - [George and Jason scheduling refinement call with Thiago](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=868.0)
  - [George sent Notion page outlining process and deliverables](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=879.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Monitor SEO performance, especially click data discrepancies in Search Console](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=171.0)
  - [Vivek to follow up with Tejas on pending website updates in a couple of days](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=955.0)
  - [Mara to bump Tiago for review of JTBD memo and schedule follow-up session if needed](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=976.0)
  - [Continue refining content strategy based on JTBD insights once available](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
  - [Kaitlin to provide support on website dev if Tejas continues to face delays](https://fathom.video/share/m26zhwkENvxaSMt1HoqzhqKUSnx3m6mS?tab=summary&timestamp=937.0)

action_items.description: Bump Tiago re review of JTBD memo. Schedule follow-up session if needed.
